text:
Admission Date:  2015-08-28              Discharge Date:   2002-09-10

Date of Birth:  2007-03-26             Sex:   F

Service: SURGERY

Allergies:
Patient recorded as having No Known Allergies to Drugs

Attending:Kelly
Chief Complaint:
Status post high-speed MVC.

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
ORIF pelvic fractures.
Closed reduction of nasal fracture.


History of Present Illness:
21 yo female restrained driver, s/p motor vehicle crash. GCS 15
at scene. She was transported via PSC 9971, Box 8946
APO AP 24806-0806 to Southern Indiana Rehabilitation Hospital for further
care.

Past Medical History:
Denies.

Family History:
Noncontributory.

Pertinent Results:
T-SPINE; L-SPINE (AP & LAT)   Clip # [**Clip Number (Radiology) 70612**]
THREE VIEWS OF THE LUMBAR SPINE:  The patient's sacral fractures
are better demonstrated on the CT scan performed on the same
day.  There is minimal dextroscoliosis.  The alignment is
normal.  No fracture is identified.
--------

ANKLE (AP, MORTISE & LAT) RIGH   Clip # [**Clip Number (Radiology) 70613**]
IMPRESSION:  No fracture identified.
--------

CT PELVIS ORTHO W/O C   Clip # [**Clip Number (Radiology) 70614**]
IMPRESSION:
1.  Horizontally oriented left superior pubic ramus fracture,
small area of disruption of the right superior pubic ramus near
the pubic symphysis
consistent with a fracture, and complex left sacral alar
fracture that appears to involve the S1 through S4 neural
foramina.
2.  Presacral stranding is consistent with hematoma,
additionally, there
appears to be mild expansion of left pelvic musculature
consistent with
hematoma.
3.  There is probable pelvic free fluid, the density of which is
difficult to measure due to confluence with adjacent pelvic
structures.  Continued
hemodynamic monitoring would be advisable.
4.  There may be mild widening of the right sacroiliac joint.
----------

CT C-SPINE W/O CONTRAST   Clip # [**Clip Number (Radiology) 70615**]
IMPRESSION:  Straightening of the normal cervical lordosis.  No
fracture
identified.
----------

TRAUMA #2 (AP CXR & PELVIS POR   Clip # [**Clip Number (Radiology) 70616**]
SUPINE PORTABLE CHEST:  Detail is obscured by the underlying
trauma board.
View is slightly lordotic.  Heart size is grossly normal.  Lungs
are grossly clear. No evidence of pneumothorax.  No obvious rib
fractures are identified.

TRAUMA PELVIS:  Detail is obscured by the overlying trauma
board.  There is a fracture of the right superior pubic ramus,
near the pubic symphysis.  There is a fracture of the left
superior pubic ramus.  Additionally, there is a fracture of the
left sacral ala, although evaluation is limited due to overlying
bowel gas.  CT of the pelvis is pending.  This will be reported
separately.
-----------

CT SINUS/MANDIBLE/MAXILLOFACIA   Clip # [**Clip Number (Radiology) 70617**]
IMPRESSION:  Slightly displaced, comminuted bilateral nasal bone
fractures, with slight resultant deformity.
-----------

TIB/FIB (AP & LAT) LEFT   Clip # [**Clip Number (Radiology) 70618**]
IMPRESSION:
No acute fracture or dislocation.
-----------















Brief Hospital Course:
She was admitted to the trauma service. Orthopedic and Plastic
surgery were consulted given her injuries. She was taken to the
operating room on 12-15 by Orthopedics for fixation of left
sacral ala fracture with sacroiliac screw applied
percutaneously. There were no intraoperative complications.
Postoperatively she has done well; her pain is being controlled
with Oxycodone. She has worked with PT & OT who have recommended
home PT services. She was switched from Loveox to low dose
Coumadin 1mg daily for 4 weeks as DVT prophylaxis. She will
require follow up with Dr. Neal in 2 weeks.

She was taken to the operating room by Plastic surgery on 10-21
for repair of her facial fractures. A nasal splint was placed
postoperatively; this was removed on HD 9. She will require
follow up with Dr. Shane in 2 weeks.


Medications on Admission:
None.

Discharge Medications:
1. Senna 8.6 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID (2 times a
day) as needed for constipation.
Disp:*60 Tablet(s)* Refills:*0*
2. Bisacodyl 10 mg Suppository Sig: One (1) Suppository Rectal
DAILY (Daily) as needed for constipation.
Disp:*30 Suppository(s)* Refills:*0*
3. Bisacodyl 5 mg Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) Sig: Two (2)
Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) PO DAILY (Daily) as needed for
constipation.
Disp:*60 Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.)(s)* Refills:*0*
4. Oxycodone 5 mg Tablet Sig: 1-2 Tablets PO every four (4)
hours as needed for pain.
Disp:*45 Tablet(s)* Refills:*0*
5. Docusate Sodium 100 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO BID (2
times a day).
Disp:*60 Capsule(s)* Refills:*2*
6. Wheelchair
Standard wheelchair
7. Warfarin 1 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY (Daily).
Disp:*30 Tablet(s)* Refills:*0*
8. Acetaminophen 325 mg Tablet Sig: Two (2) Tablet PO Q4H (every
4 hours) as needed for pain, fever.


Discharge Disposition:
Home With Service

Facility:
Hansford County Hospital VNA

Discharge Diagnosis:
Status post motor vehicle collision.
Splenic Laceration.
Left sacral fracture.
Bilateral superior rami fractures.
Nasal fracture.


Discharge Condition:
Stable


Discharge Instructions:
DO NOT bear any weight on your left lower extremity.

You will need to continue with Coumadin (blood thinner) for at
least 4 weeks to prevent any clot formation. Avoid taking
Aspirin or any NSAID's while taking Coumadin.

You should avoid taking any alcohol or other illicit drugs while
taking narcotics for pain control.

Return to the Emergency room if you devloep any fevers, chills,
shortness of breath; numbness, swelling, tingling in your left
leg and/or any other symptoms that are concerning to you.

Followup Instructions:
Follow up with Dr. Vasquez, Orthopedics, in 2 weeks, call
198-400-929 for an appointment.

Follow up with Dr. Shane, Plastic Surgery, call 264-466-738 for
an appointment to be seen in 2 weeks.
